attributes.io.input:
{"description":"Explore project structure","prompt":"Explore the project structure at /home/user/workspace. I need to understand:
1. What framework is being used (Next.js?)
2. Where posts-related code lives
3. How posts are currently listed and cached
4. Any existing server actions or API routes

List all relevant files and show their contents.","subagent_type":"Explore"}
attributes.io.output:
[{"type":"text","text":"Here is a complete summary of the project. --- ## Project Structure Report ### 1. Framework: Next.js (Canary) with React 19 This is a **Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41** project using the **App Router** (`/app` directory). It uses: - TypeScript with strict mode - React 19.1.0 - `next.config.ts` — minimal configuration (empty object) - `@/*` path alias pointing to project root - Vitest is listed in devDependencies but no config or test files exist yet **Key files:** - `/home/user/workspace/package.json` - `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` - `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` ### 2. Posts-Related Code The app only has two source files, and both reference "Posts": **`/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx`** -- Root layout with metadata title "Posts" and description "Create and view posts". **`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx`** -- The home page renders a placeholder: ```tsx export default function Home() { return (

Posts

Please implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation.

); } ``` There is **no** actual posts data model, no posts fetching logic, and no posts display component yet. The page is essentially a stub/template waiting for implementation. ### 3. How Posts Are Currently Listed and Cached Posts are **not listed or cached at all**. There is: - No data fetching (`fetch`, `getPosts`, etc.) - No database client or ORM - No cache layer (no `unstable_cache`, no `cache()`, no revalidation) - No posts data structure or type definitions - No state management ### 4. Server Actions or API Routes **None exist.** Specifically: - No `"use server"` actions (no files with that directive) - No `app/api/` directory - No route handlers (`route.ts` files) - No form actions ### 5. Git History The repo has two commits: - `4388e3d` — "baseline" (initial setup) - `f5c65f7` — "workspace" (updated placeholder content in `layout.tsx`, `page.tsx`, and config files) --- ## All Relevant Files (Complete List) | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `/home/user/workspace/package.json` | Dependencies: Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41, React 19.1.0, TypeScript, Vitest | | `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` | Empty Next.js config | | `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config with `@/*` alias | | `/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx` | Root layout, metadata: title="Posts", desc="Create and view posts" | | `/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx` | Home page stub instructing to implement a Server Action for posts | | `/home/user/workspace/.gitignore` | Standard Next.js gitignore | **Bottom line:** This is a bare Next.js scaffold. There is no posts implementation whatsoever -- no data layer, no API routes, no server actions, no caching, no listing. The app displays a single page that says "Posts" with a placeholder telling the developer to implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation."}]

[{"type":"text","text":"Here is a complete summary of the project. --- ## Project Structure Report ### 1. Framework: Next.js (Canary) with React 19 This is a **Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41** project using the **App Router** (`/app` directory). It uses: - TypeScript with strict mode - React 19.1.0 - `next.config.ts` — minimal configuration (empty object) - `@/*` path alias pointing to project root - Vitest is listed in devDependencies but no config or test files exist yet **Key files:** - `/home/user/workspace/package.json` - `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` - `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` ### 2. Posts-Related Code The app only has two source files, and both reference "Posts": **`/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx`** -- Root layout with metadata title "Posts" and description "Create and view posts". **`/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx`** -- The home page renders a placeholder: ```tsx export default function Home() { return (

Posts

Please implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation.

); } ``` There is **no** actual posts data model, no posts fetching logic, and no posts display component yet. The page is essentially a stub/template waiting for implementation. ### 3. How Posts Are Currently Listed and Cached Posts are **not listed or cached at all**. There is: - No data fetching (`fetch`, `getPosts`, etc.) - No database client or ORM - No cache layer (no `unstable_cache`, no `cache()`, no revalidation) - No posts data structure or type definitions - No state management ### 4. Server Actions or API Routes **None exist.** Specifically: - No `"use server"` actions (no files with that directive) - No `app/api/` directory - No route handlers (`route.ts` files) - No form actions ### 5. Git History The repo has two commits: - `4388e3d` — "baseline" (initial setup) - `f5c65f7` — "workspace" (updated placeholder content in `layout.tsx`, `page.tsx`, and config files) --- ## All Relevant Files (Complete List) | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `/home/user/workspace/package.json` | Dependencies: Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41, React 19.1.0, TypeScript, Vitest | | `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` | Empty Next.js config | | `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config with `@/*` alias | | `/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx` | Root layout, metadata: title="Posts", desc="Create and view posts" | | `/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx` | Home page stub instructing to implement a Server Action for posts | | `/home/user/workspace/.gitignore` | Standard Next.js gitignore | **Bottom line:** This is a bare Next.js scaffold. There is no posts implementation whatsoever -- no data layer, no API routes, no server actions, no caching, no listing. The app displays a single page that says "Posts" with a placeholder telling the developer to implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation."}]

1	'use server'
     2	
     3	import { revalidateTag } from 'next/cache'
     4	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
     5	import { addPost } from '@/app/lib/data'
     6	
     7	export async function createPost(formData: FormData) {
     8	  const title = formData.get('title') as string
     9	  const body = formData.get('body') as string
    10	
    11	  if (!title || !body) {
    12	    throw new Error('Title and body are required')
    13	  }
    14	
    15	  addPost(title, body)
    16	
    17	  // Invalidate the "posts" cache tag so the next page load fetches fresh
    18	  // data instead of serving stale cached content.
    19	  revalidateTag('posts')
    20	
    21	  // Redirect to the posts page, triggering a server-side render that will
    22	  // call getPosts() — because the "posts" tag was just invalidated, the
    23	  // cache miss forces a fresh data fetch before the page is served.
    24	  redirect('/')
    25	}

attributes.io.output:
1	'use server'
2	
3	import { updateTag } from 'next/cache'
4	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
5	import { addPost } from '@/app/lib/data'
6	
7	export async function createPost(formData: FormData) {
8	  const title = formData.get('title') as string
9	  const body = formData.get('body') as string
10	
11	  if (!title || !body) {
12	    throw new Error('Title and body are required')
13	  }
14	
15	  addPost(title, body)
16	
17	  // updateTag invalidates the "posts" cache tag with read-your-own-writes
18	  // semantics — it immediately expires the cache and marks the route as
19	  // revalidated, guaranteeing the next render fetches fresh data.
20	  // In this canary build, revalidateTag requires a second argument (a cache
21	  // profile string like "max" or a CacheLifeConfig), while updateTag is the
22	  // idiomatic choice inside a Server Action: single argument, immediate
23	  // expiration, no deprecation warning.
24	  updateTag('posts')
25	
26	  // Redirect to the home page, triggering a server-side render. Because
27	  // updateTag('posts') just invalidated the cache, the getPosts() call
28	  // during render will miss the cache and fetch fresh data.
29	  redirect('/')
30	}
31

1	'use server'
2	
3	import { updateTag } from 'next/cache'
4	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
5	import { addPost } from '@/app/lib/data'
6	
7	export async function createPost(formData: FormData) {
8	  const title = formData.get('title') as string
9	  const body = formData.get('body') as string
10	
11	  if (!title || !body) {
12	    throw new Error('Title and body are required')
13	  }
14	
15	  addPost(title, body)
16	
17	  // updateTag invalidates the "posts" cache tag with read-your-own-writes
18	  // semantics — it immediately expires the cache and marks the route as
19	  // revalidated, guaranteeing the next render fetches fresh data.
20	  // In this canary build, revalidateTag requires a second argument (a cache
21	  // profile string like "max" or a CacheLifeConfig), while updateTag is the
22	  // idiomatic choice inside a Server Action: single argument, immediate
23	  // expiration, no deprecation warning.
24	  updateTag('posts')
25	
26	  // Redirect to the home page, triggering a server-side render. Because
27	  // updateTag('posts') just invalidated the cache, the getPosts() call
28	  // during render will miss the cache and fetch fresh data.
29	  redirect('/')
30	}
31

attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.output:
1	import { unstable_cache } from 'next/cache'
2	
3	export type Post = {
4	  id: number
5	  title: string
6	  body: string
7	}
8	
9	const store: Post[] = [
10	  { id: 1, title: 'First Post', body: 'This is the first post.' },
11	  { id: 2, title: 'Second Post', body: 'This is the second post.' },
12	]
13	
14	/**
15	 * Returns all posts, cached with the "posts" tag so it can be invalidated
16	 * on demand via revalidateTag("posts").
17	 */
18	export const getPosts = unstable_cache(
19	  async (): Promise<Post[]> => {
20	    return [...store]
21	  },
22	  ['posts-list'],
23	  { tags: ['posts'] },
24	)
25	
26	/**
27	 * Adds a new post to the in-memory store.
28	 */
29	export function addPost(title: string, body: string): Post {
30	  const id = store.length + 1
31	  const post: Post = { id, title, body }
32	  store.push(post)
33	  return post
34	}
35

6	  body: string

10	  { id: 1, title: 'First Post', body: 'This is the first post.' },
11	  { id: 2, title: 'Second Post', body: 'This is the second post.' },
12	]
13	
14	/**
15	 * Returns all posts, cached with the "posts" tag so it can be invalidated
16	 * on demand via revalidateTag("posts").
17	 */
18	export const getPosts = unstable_cache(
19	  async (): Promise<Post[]> => {

21	  },

24	)
25

30	  const id = store.length + 1
31	  const post: Post = { id, title, body }
32	  store.push(post)
33	  return post
